heading: Requirements for certain mortgages
label: 1639 Requirements for certain mortgages

children.label: A
children.text: “You are not required to complete this agreement merely because you have received these disclosures or have signed a loan application.”.

children.label: B
children.text: “If you obtain this loan, the lender will have a mortgage on your home. You could lose your home, and any money you have put into it, if you do not meet your obligations under the loan.”.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a credit transaction with a fixed rate of interest, the annual percentage rate and the amount of the regular monthly payment; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any other credit transaction, the annual percentage rate of the loan, the amount of the regular monthly payment, a statement that the interest rate and monthly payment may increase, and the amount of the maximum monthly payment, based on the maximum interest rate allowed pursuant to .section 3806 of title 12

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The disclosures required by this section shall be given not less than 3 business days prior to consummation of the transaction.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: After providing the disclosures required by this section, a creditor may not change the terms of the extension of credit if such changes make the disclosures inaccurate, unless new disclosures are provided that meet the requirements of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the change is initiated by the consumer; and

children.label: I
children.text: the creditor provides to the consumer the new disclosures, in writing; and

children.label: II
children.text: the creditor and consumer certify in writing that the new disclosures were provided by telephone, by not later than 3 days prior to the date of consummation of the transaction.

children.heading: No wait for lower rate
children.label: 3 No wait for lower rate
children.text: If a creditor extends to a consumer a second offer of credit with a lower annual percentage rate, the transaction may be consummated without regard to the period specified in paragraph (1) with respect to the second offer.

children.heading: Modifications
children.label: 4 Modifications
children.text: The Bureau may, if it finds that such action is necessary to permit homeowners to meet bona fide personal financial emergencies, prescribe regulations authorizing the modification or waiver of rights created under this subsection, to the extent and under the circumstances set forth in those regulations.

children.heading: In general2So in original. There is no par. (2).2
children.label: 1 In general2So in original. There is no par. (2).2

children.heading: Limitation on terms
children.label: A Limitation on terms
children.text: A mortgage referred to in section 1602(aa) of this title may not contain terms under which a consumer must pay a prepayment penalty for paying all or part of the principal before the date on which the principal is due.1

children.heading: Construction
children.label: B Construction
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, any method of computing a refund of unearned scheduled interest is a prepayment penalty if it is less favorable to the consumer than the actuarial method (as that term is defined in ).section 1615(d) of this title

children.heading: Limitations after default
children.label: d Limitations after default
children.text: A mortgage referred to in section 1602(aa) of this title may not provide for an interest rate applicable after default that is higher than the interest rate that applies before default. If the date of maturity of a mortgage referred to in subsection 1602(aa) of this title is accelerated due to default and the consumer is entitled to a rebate of interest, that rebate shall be computed by any method that is not less favorable than the actuarial method (as that term is defined in ).113section 1615(d) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “section”.3

children.heading: No balloon payments
children.label: e No balloon payments
children.text: No high-cost mortgage may contain a scheduled payment that is more than twice as large as the average of earlier scheduled payments. This subsection shall not apply when the payment schedule is adjusted to the seasonal or irregular income of the consumer.

children.heading: No negative amortization
children.label: f No negative amortization
children.text: A mortgage referred to in section 1602(aa) of this title may not include terms under which the outstanding principal balance will increase at any time over the course of the loan because the regular periodic payments do not cover the full amount of interest due.1

children.heading: No prepaid payments
children.label: g No prepaid payments
children.text: A mortgage referred to in section 1602(aa) of this title may not include terms under which more than 2 periodic payments required under the loan are consolidated and paid in advance from the loan proceeds provided to the consumer.1

children.heading: Prohibition on extending credit without regard to payment ability of consumer
children.label: h Prohibition on extending credit without regard to payment ability of consumer
children.text: A creditor shall not engage in a pattern or practice of extending credit to consumers under mortgages referred to in section 1602(aa) of this title based on the consumers’ collateral without regard to the consumers’ repayment ability, including the consumers’ current and expected income, current obligations, and employment.1

children.heading: Requirements for payments under home improvement contracts
children.label: i Requirements for payments under home improvement contracts

children.text: in the form of an instrument that is payable to the consumer or jointly to the consumer and the contractor; or

at the election of the consumer, by a third party escrow agent in accordance with terms established in a written agreement signed by the consumer, the creditor, and the contractor before the date of payment.

children.heading: Recommended default
children.label: j Recommended default
children.text: No creditor shall recommend or encourage default on an existing loan or other debt prior to and in connection with the closing or planned closing of a high-cost mortgage that refinances all or any portion of such existing loan or debt.

children.label: A
children.text: in an amount in excess of 4 percent of the amount of the payment past due;

children.label: B
children.text: unless the loan documents specifically authorize the charge or fee;

children.label: C
children.text: before the end of the 15-day period beginning on the date the payment is due, or in the case of a loan on which interest on each installment is paid in advance, before the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date the payment is due; or

children.label: D
children.text: more than once with respect to a single late payment.

children.heading: Coordination with subsequent late fees
children.label: 2 Coordination with subsequent late fees
children.text: If a payment is otherwise a full payment for the applicable period and is paid on its due date or within an applicable grace period, and the only delinquency or insufficiency of payment is attributable to any late fee or delinquency charge assessed on any earlier payment, no late fee or delinquency charge may be imposed on such payment.

children.heading: Failure to make installment payment
children.label: 3 Failure to make installment payment
children.text: If, in the case of a loan agreement the terms of which provide that any payment shall first be applied to any past due principal balance, the consumer fails to make an installment payment and the consumer subsequently resumes making installment payments but has not paid all past due installments, the creditor may impose a separate late payment charge or fee for any principal due (without deduction due to late fees or related fees) until the default is cured.

children.heading: Acceleration of debt
children.label: l Acceleration of debt
children.text: No high-cost mortgage may contain a provision which permits the creditor to accelerate the indebtedness, except when repayment of the loan has been accelerated by default in payment, or pursuant to a due-on-sale provision, or pursuant to a material violation of some other provision of the loan document unrelated to payment schedule.

children.heading: Restriction on financing points and fees
children.label: m Restriction on financing points and fees

children.text: Any prepayment fee or penalty payable by the consumer in a refinancing transaction if the creditor or an affiliate of the creditor is the noteholder of the note being refinanced.
Any points or fees.

children.heading: Consequence of failure to comply
children.label: n Consequence of failure to comply
children.text: Any mortgage that contains a provision prohibited by this section shall be deemed a failure to deliver the material disclosures required under this subchapter, for the purpose of .section 1635 of this title

children.heading: “Affiliate” defined
children.label: o “Affiliate” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “affiliate” has the same meaning as in .section 1841(k) of title 12

children.heading: Discretionary regulatory authority of Bureau
children.label: p Discretionary regulatory authority of Bureau
A
children.text: is in the interest of the borrowing public; and

children.label: B
children.text: will apply only to products that maintain and strengthen home ownership and equity protection.

children.label: A
children.text: mortgage loans that the Bureau finds to be unfair, deceptive, or designed to evade the provisions of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: refinancing of mortgage loans that the Bureau finds to be associated with abusive lending practices, or that are otherwise not in the interest of the borrower.

children.heading: Civil penalties in Federal Trade Commission enforcement actions
children.label: q Civil penalties in Federal Trade Commission enforcement actions
children.text: For purposes of enforcement by the Federal Trade Commission, any violation of a regulation issued by the Bureau pursuant to subsection ()(2) shall be treated as a violation of a rule promulgated under regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices.lsection 57a of this title

children.heading: Prohibitions on evasions, structuring of transactions, and reciprocal arrangements
children.label: r Prohibitions on evasions, structuring of transactions, and reciprocal arrangements

children.text: to structure a loan transaction as an open-end credit plan or another form of loan for the purpose and with the intent of evading the provisions of this subchapter; or
to divide any loan transaction into separate parts for the purpose and with the intent of evading provisions of this subchapter.

children.heading: Modification and deferral fees prohibited
children.label: s Modification and deferral fees prohibited
children.text: A creditor, successor in interest, assignee, or any agent of any of the above, may not charge a consumer any fee to modify, renew, extend, or amend a high-cost mortgage, or to defer any payment due under the terms of such mortgage.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), no creditor or servicer may charge a fee for informing or transmitting to any person the balance due to pay off the outstanding balance on a high-cost mortgage.

children.heading: Transaction fee
children.label: B Transaction fee
children.text: When payoff information referred to in subparagraph (A) is provided by facsimile transmission or by a courier service, a creditor or servicer may charge a processing fee to cover the cost of such transmission or service in an amount not to exceed an amount that is comparable to fees imposed for similar services provided in connection with consumer credit transactions that are secured by the consumer’s principal dwelling and are not high-cost mortgages.

children.heading: Fee disclosure
children.label: C Fee disclosure
children.text: Prior to charging a transaction fee as provided in subparagraph (B), a creditor or servicer shall disclose that payoff balances are available for free pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Multiple requests
children.label: D Multiple requests
children.text: If a creditor or servicer has provided payoff information referred to in subparagraph (A) without charge, other than the transaction fee allowed by subparagraph (B), on 4 occasions during a calendar year, the creditor or servicer may thereafter charge a reasonable fee for providing such information during the remainder of the calendar year.

children.heading: Prompt delivery
children.label: 2 Prompt delivery
children.text: Payoff balances shall be provided within 5 business days after receiving a request by a consumer or a person authorized by the consumer to obtain such information.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A creditor may not extend credit to a consumer under a high-cost mortgage without first receiving certification from a counselor that is approved by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, or at the discretion of the Secretary, a State housing finance authority, that the consumer has received counseling on the advisability of the mortgage. Such counselor shall not be employed by the creditor or an affiliate of the creditor or be affiliated with the creditor.

children.heading: Disclosures required prior to counseling
children.label: 2 Disclosures required prior to counseling
children.text: No counselor may certify that a consumer has received counseling on the advisability of the high-cost mortgage unless the counselor can verify that the consumer has received each statement required (in connection with such loan) by this section or the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 [ et seq.] with respect to the transaction.12 U.S.C. 2601

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Board may prescribe such regulations as the Board determines to be appropriate to carry out the requirements of paragraph (1).4So in original. Probably should be “Bureau”.4

children.label: A
children.text: make the loan satisfy the requirements of this part; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a high-cost mortgage, change the terms of the loan in a manner beneficial to the consumer so that the loan will no longer be a high-cost mortgage; or

children.label: A
children.text: make the loan satisfy the requirements of this part; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a high-cost mortgage, change the terms of the loan in a manner beneficial so that the loan will no longer be a high-cost mortgage.
